title: Search Metadata, Indexing Directives, Canonical Signals, and Language Alternates · Language Clinic: Canonical Signal Reconciliation
summary: The audit equates crawl permission with indexability, trusts one canonical tag, ignores HTTP robots fields, and reports hreflang without reciprocal or language checks. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the canonical signal reconciliation increment in search discovery and indexability audit record. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Reconcile redirects, HTTP canonical links, HTML canonical links, sitemap entries, internal links, and content identity without forcing agreement.
Extract title and description candidates with cardinality, location, text quality, changed-case, and stakeholder-specific non-ranking claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-canonical-signal-reconciliation — Reconcile redirects, HTTP canonical links, HTML canonical links, sitemap entries, internal links, and content identity without forcing agreement.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-indexing-canonical-hreflang-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-indexing-canonical-hreflang-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_indexing_canonical_hreflang_3(pages: int = 36, indexing_decisions: int = 36, canonical_conflicts: int = 4, alternate_sets: int = 6, unsupported_index_guarantees: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or indexing_decisions < 0:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-count"
    if indexing_decisions != pages:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-reconciliation"
    if canonical_conflicts < 0 or alternate_sets < 1:
        return False, canonical_conflicts, "search-signal-evidence"
    if unsupported_index_guarantees > 0:
        return False, unsupported_index_guarantees, "index-guarantee"
    assert indexing_decisions == pages and unsupported_index_guarantees == 0
    return True, indexing_decisions, "evidence-based-indexability-audit"

# Evidence: scraper-canonical-signal-reconciliation-17msap
# Competency: scraper-canonical-signal-reconciliation.
# Case 8900: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_canonical_signal_reconciliation_17msap(pages: int = 36, indexing_decisions: int = 36, canonical_conflicts: int = 4, alternate_sets: int = 6, unsupported_index_guarantees: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17msap = "17msap-pasm71-8900"
    assert evidence_variant_17msap
    evidence_axes_17msap = (
        "authorization17msap",
        "url8900",
        "responsepasm71",
        "tree17msap8900",
        "frontier890017msap",
        "reportpasm718900",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17msap)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or indexing_decisions < 0:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-count"
    if indexing_decisions != pages:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-reconciliation"
    if canonical_conflicts < 0 or alternate_sets < 1:
        return False, canonical_conflicts, "search-signal-evidence"
    if unsupported_index_guarantees > 0:
        return False, unsupported_index_guarantees, "index-guarantee"
    assert indexing_decisions == pages and unsupported_index_guarantees == 0
    return True, indexing_decisions, "evidence-based-indexability-audit"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Canonical Signal Reconciliation model · Theory Canonical…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Canonical Signal Reconciliation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 8372 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile search metadata, robots directives, canonical signals, language alternates, and indexability non-claims; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reconcile redirects, HTTP canonical links, HTML canonical links, sitemap entries, internal links, and content identity without forcing agreement.
